<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# AGENTS.md — AgentReady Operating Contract

> This file is the authoritative operating contract for GitHub Copilot, OpenAI agents, and any other AI agent working in this repository. Read it completely before taking any action.

---

## Project Overview

**Project:** `agent-ready`
**Language:** Python 3 (single-package, `src/` layout)
**Build tool:** `pip` / `setuptools` with dynamic version reading
**Key runtime dependency:** `litellm` (optional — only available when installed with the `[ai]` extra)
**Linter/Formatter:** `ruff`
**Test framework:** `pytest` with `pytest-cov`

AgentReady transforms any legacy repository into an AI-agent-ready codebase by running a 5-phase pipeline that reads actual source code, sends it to an LLM for structured analysis, and then generates scaffolding files (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `system_prompt.md`, `agent-context.json`, `memory/schema.md`) that give AI agents verified, grounded knowledge of that repository. It is provider-agnostic via LiteLLM, supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Groq, Mistral, Together, and Ollama. The CLI entry point is `src/agent_ready/cli.py`; the `agent-ready` command is installed by `pip install`.

---

## Commands

These are the exact, verified commands for this repository. Use them verbatim.

### Install (development)
```bash
pip install -e '.[dev]'
```

### Install (end-user, with LLM support)
```bash
pip install "git+https://github.com/vb-nattamai/agent-ready.git[ai]"
```

### Run
```bash
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo
```

### Run (dry-run — preview without writing any files)
```bash
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo --dry-run
```

### Run (evaluate existing context files without regenerating)
```bash
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo --eval-only
```

### Test (with coverage enforcement)
```bash
pytest -q --cov=src/agent_ready --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=50
```

### Lint
```bash
ruff check .
```

### Format check (non-destructive)
```bash
ruff format --check .
```

### Format apply
```bash
ruff format .
```

> **Note:** `PYTHONPATH` may need to be set to the repo root when running tests outside of an installed environment: `PYTHONPATH=. pytest ...`

---

## Safe Operations

An agent is explicitly permitted to perform the following operations without seeking additional approval:

- Run `pytest -q --cov=src/agent_ready --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=50` to verify tests pass.
- Run `ruff check .` to check linting status.
- Run `ruff format --check .` to verify formatting without modifying files.
- Run `agent-ready --dry-run` to preview generated output without writing any files to disk.
- Run `agent-ready --eval-only` to assess existing context files without triggering regeneration.
- Read any file under `src/agent_ready/` to understand implementation.
- Read any file under `tests/` to understand test patterns and fixtures.
- Modify test files under `tests/` to add or improve test coverage.
- Add new source files under `src/agent_ready/` (following `snake_case` naming).
- Modify `CHANGELOG.md` or `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- Modify `README.md` for documentation improvements.

---

## Forbidden Operations

The following operations are **absolutely prohibited**. Violating these will break the project, the release pipeline, or its security boundaries.

### Restricted write paths — never modify these files under any circumstances:
- `LICENSE` — immutable legal artifact.
- `VERSION` — managed exclusively by the release workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`); editing by hand will break version synchronisation.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — CI pipeline; changes require explicit team approval.
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — release automation; changes require explicit team approval.
- `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` — security scanning; changes require explicit team approval.

### Additional hard prohibitions:
- **Never commit API keys or secrets** to any file, including test fixtures, comments, or `.env` examples.
- **Never modify `.github/workflows/` files** without explicit written approval — this includes `install-to-target-repo.yml`, `reusable-transformer.yml`, and `context-drift-detector.yml`.
- **Never use `shell=True`** in any `subprocess` call anywhere in the codebase. All subprocess invocations must use argument lists for security.
- **Never edit files in `src/agent_ready/templates/`** without first understanding the `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` syntax used there (see Pitfall #4 below).
- **Never modify the version string in `pyproject.toml`** directly. Version is read dynamically from `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` via setuptools; edit only `__init__.py` and let the release workflow sync `VERSION`.
- **Never push directly to `main`**. All changes must go through pull requests.

---

## Domain Glossary

These terms have precise meanings in this codebase. Use them as defined — do not substitute synonyms.

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **agent-context.json** | Machine-readable JSON file containing both static (user-editable) and dynamic (LLM-generated) metadata about a repository, used to ground AI agents in verified facts about the codebase. |
| **scaffolding files** | The up-to-12 files AgentReady generates: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `system_prompt.md`, `agent-context.json`, `mcp.json`, `memory/schema.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `tools/refresh_context.py`, `.github/dependabot.yml`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `openapi.yaml` (REST APIs), `.agent-ready/custom_questions.json`. |
| **context drift** | The condition where generated scaffolding files have become stale relative to actual code changes in the target repository. Detected weekly by the `context-drift-detector.yml` workflow. |
| **readiness score** | A 100-point score computed in Phase 4 measuring how well-prepared a codebase is for AI agent interaction. |
| **provider preset** | A named configuration (e.g. `anthropic`, `openai`, `google`) that maps the three pipeline phases (analysis, generation, evaluation) to specific LLM model strings. |
| **Phase 1 — Collect** | The mechanical, LLM-free phase that reads the file tree, source files, config files, CI config, and README from the target repository. No LLM reasoning occurs here. |
| **Phase 2 — Analyse** | The LLM-powered phase that sends collected code to a model (strongest tier) and receives structured JSON describing the repository's architecture, components, and pitfalls. |
| **Phase 3 — Generate** | The LLM-powered phase that takes the analysis JSON and writes each scaffolding output file from scratch. |
| **Phase 5 — Evaluate** | The assessment phase that loads a versioned golden question set (13 base + language overlay = 13–19 questions), extracts ground truth from raw source code (not context files) using a haiku-class model, runs a weak-model baseline for comparison, applies a 3-judge panel for context responses, and measures hallucination rate. |
| **restricted_write_paths** | Paths that AI agents must never modify, identified during analysis and recorded in `agent-context.json`. The list for this repo is: `LICENSE`, `VERSION`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`. |
| **workflow templates** | YAML workflow files in `src/agent_ready/templates/` installed into target repos by the installer (`agentic-ready.yml`, `context-drift-detector.yml`, `pr-review.yml`, `agentic-ready-eval.yml`). These are NOT language-specific MCP tool scaffolds — no Python/Java/Go tool files exist here. |

---

## Key Components

Each component below is a discrete unit of responsibility. Understand which component owns a concern before editing it.

### CLI
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/cli.py`
- **Responsibility:** Main entry point and argument parser. Orchestrates the full 5-phase pipeline, manages provider selection and model override logic, and handles `--dry-run`, `--eval-only`, and `--review` modes. This is where the `agent-ready` command begins execution.

### Analyser
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/analyser.py`
- **Responsibility:** Implements Phase 1 (Collect) and Phase 2 (Analyse). Phase 1 reads repository files mechanically without any LLM. Phase 2 sends collected code to an LLM to produce the structured analysis JSON that feeds all downstream phases. This module maintains `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` — the set of AgentReady-generated files that must never be fed back into analysis.

### Generator
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/generator.py`
- **Responsibility:** Implements Phase 3 (Generate). Accepts the analysis JSON produced by the Analyser and uses an LLM to write up to 12 scaffolding files: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `system_prompt.md`, `agent-context.json`, `mcp.json`, `memory/schema.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `tools/refresh_context.py`, `.github/dependabot.yml`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `openapi.yaml` (REST APIs), `.agent-ready/custom_questions.json`. Each file is written from scratch on every run.

### Evaluator
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/evaluator.py`
- **Responsibility:** Implements Phase 5 (Evaluate). Loads a versioned golden question set (13 base + language overlay = 13–19 questions), extracts non-circular ground truth from raw source code via `ground_truth.py`, runs a weak-model (haiku-class) baseline, applies a 3-judge panel for context responses, and measures hallucination rate. Produces `AGENTIC_EVAL.md`.

### Ground Truth Extractor
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/ground_truth.py`
- **Responsibility:** Extracts ground truth for each eval question from raw source files (not generated context). Uses a haiku-class model for extraction to prevent circular self-reference. This is what makes the v2 eval non-circular.

### MCP Server
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/mcp_server.py`
- **Responsibility:** Exposes AgentReady as an MCP server with four tools: `transform`, `score`, `evaluate`, `review_pr`. Started via the `agent-ready-mcp` command. 98% test coverage.

### Reviewer
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/reviewer.py`
- **Responsibility:** PR review module. Reviews pull requests using LLM reasoning grounded in `agent-context.json`, then posts structured review comments back to GitHub via the GitHub API. Treats all PR diff content as untrusted input — it is sent to LLM APIs but never executed locally.

### Workflow Templates
- **Path:** `src/agent_ready/templates/`
- **Responsibility:** YAML workflow templates installed into target repos by the installer. Contains `agentic-ready.yml`, `context-drift-detector.yml`, `pr-review.yml`, and `agentic-ready-eval.yml`. **These are NOT language-specific MCP tool stubs.** No Python/Java/Go/TypeScript tool files exist here.

### Install Workflow
- **Path:** `.github/workflows/install-to-target-repo.yml`
- **Responsibility:** GitHub Actions workflow that pushes trigger workflows and issue templates into a target repository to bootstrap AgentReady integration. Do not modify without explicit approval.

### Reusable Transformer
- **Path:** `.github/workflows/reusable-transformer.yml`
- **Responsibility:** Reusable GitHub Actions workflow called by target repositories. Runs the full transformation pipeline against the caller's repository and opens a pull request containing all generated scaffolding files.

### Drift Detector
- **Path:** `.github/workflows/context-drift-detector.yml`
- **Responsibility:** Weekly scheduled workflow that detects meaningful drift between generated context files and the actual current state of the codebase, alerting maintainers when scaffolding has gone stale.

---

## Potential Pitfalls

> **This section is critical.** Each pitfall below describes something that appears reasonable but will break this codebase. Read every item before writing or running code.

---

### Pitfall 1 — Missing `[ai]` extra causes `ImportError` at runtime

**What the agent will try to do:** Install the package with `pip install agent-ready` (no extras) and then attempt to call any function that uses LiteLLM.

**Why it breaks things:** The core package declares zero hard dependencies and uses only the Python standard library. `litellm` is only installed when the `[ai]` extra is requested. Any code path that imports `litellm` — which includes `analyser.py`, `generator.py`, `evaluator.py`, and `reviewer.py` — will raise an `ImportError` if `litellm` is absent.

**What to do instead:** Always install with the `[ai]` extra for any work involving LLM features: `pip install "git+https://github.com/vb-nattamai/agent-ready.git[ai]"`. For development: `pip install -e '.[dev]'` which includes all extras.

---

### Pitfall
